title: Gooey Saudi-Arabian Fish Tagine
description: A rich, pescatarian-friendly fish tagine from Saudi Arabia, featuring sweet red peppers and a hint of shio-koji cure.
instructions: Season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the salmon fillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until browned.
Transfer the salmon to a plate and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot.
Add the sliced red bell peppers and cook for 3-4 minutes, until tender.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes, until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Return the salmon fillet to the pot and stir in the panko breadcrumbs.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 8-10 minutes, until the fish is cooked through and the sauce is bubbly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and season with salt and pepper to taste.
